commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual review is required because the protected draft adds persisted marketplace config and trust-boundary surface, with an incomplete configured-bridge proof path but no narrow automation repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain defect was found in the diff; the remaining security concern is maintainer acceptance of the marketplace trust-root boundary before activation. (sha=8b6fa2f51aa493bdd69dd569067bca108a9113d5)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` config types, schema/help metadata, tests, and a bridge that passes `config.marketplaces` into the hosted external plugin catalog loader.

PR surface: Source +121, Tests +172. Total +293 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new hosted marketplace config feature slice, not a bug report with a current-main failure. The relevant checks are schema/loader inspection, focused tests, and real configured-profile proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is required because the protected draft adds persisted marketplace config and trust-boundary surface, with an incomplete configured-bridge proof path but no narrow automation repair.

target.title: Add hosted catalog config profiles
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## SummaryThis is PR6 in the hosted feeds stack. It wires the local OpenClaw config surface for marketplace feed profiles and local package source profiles, then adds a small bridge so the hosted external plugin catalog loader can consume `config.marketplaces` instead of taking only ad hoc in-memory profile config.This sits on PR5's live ClawHub defaults: `clawhub-public` points at `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`, `public-clawhub` represents ClawHub candidates, and `public-npm` remains available for npm-backed candidates. The config schema examples use that shape so reviewers can see how tenant or organization marketplace profiles would extend the same model. Source profiles intentionally carry only a local source name and type in this slice; registry/base URL endpoints are rejected until installer resolution can enforce them.This update also addresses Patrick's naming/API-shape feedback by using marketplace terminology for the persisted config surface: `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources`, rather than `catalog.feeds` and `catalog.sources`.This intentionally does not implement product activation of configured feeds, feed refresh scheduling, feed auth execution, signed feed verification, or installer registry switching. Auth and signed verification are rejected by this config schema until the corresponding loader/envelope enforcement exists. The purpose of this PR is to make the RFC's local ownership model concrete: feeds and sources are local config, while remote feed entries can only refer to configured source names.## What changed- Adds `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` config types and Zod schema validation.- Supports the concrete local fields used by this stack today: feed URL, refresh preferences, unsigned verification opt-in, and npm/ClawHub/Git source profile names/types.- Rejects feed auth, signed verification config, and source endpoint fields for now so users cannot configure security or installer contracts the loader does not yet enforce.- Adds config labels/help so the new section appears coherently in generated schema/docs surfaces.- Adds `loadConfiguredHostedOfficialExternalPluginCatalogEntries(...)` as the bridge from OpenClaw config to the hosted catalog loader introduced by the earlier stack.## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/types.marketplaces.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/types.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/types.marketplaces.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/types.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts`- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`  ## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
OpenClaw config can provide a named hosted marketplace feed profile and local source profiles, and the hosted loader uses that profile to load the live ClawHub feed.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr3` at pushed PR6 head `8b6fa2f51aa4`, source harness via `node --import tsx`, live HTTPS request to `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Set catalog config with `feeds.clawhub.url = https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.
2. Set source profiles `clawhub.type = clawhub` and `npm.type = npm`.
3. Ran `loadHostedOfficialExternalPluginCatalogEntries({ feedProfile: "clawhub", catalogConfig, snapshotStore: null })`.
4. Final stack validation passed formatter, lint, `git diff --check`, and focused feed/marketplace/config tests.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "head": "8b6fa2f51aa4",
  "resultSource": "hosted",
  "metadata": { "url": "https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins", "status": 200, "checksum": "sha256:90a122c19017ef2c7a0607c8c1cb893ae7871537fe649d39080a52547bed0a7f" },
  "entryCount": 59,
  "feed": { "id": "clawhub-official", "sequence": 11 },
  "firstEntries": [
    { "id": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw", "npmSpec": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw@1.0.4", "clawhubSpec": "clawhub:@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw@1.0.4" }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The configured feed profile loaded the live hosted feed as `hosted`, validated configured source refs, and produced 59 entries with resolved ClawHub and npm install specs.

What was not tested:
Signed-feed enforcement and authenticated private feed profiles are intentionally later slices.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
